name: 0xp47.github.io
description: A premium, SEO-optimized developer portfolio built with Next.js (App Router), Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion.
readme:
# Jay Patrick Cano (0xp47) вҖ” Portfolio

My personal developer portfolio built with Next.js (App Router), Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. It showcases my projects, work experience, and developer journey, optimized for clean performance and readability.

---

## рҹҡҖ Key Features

* **Modular Architecture**: Clean separation of concerns with components structured by logical usage.
* **Search & AI Discoverability**:
  * **Structured Data**: JSON-LD Schema (`Person` entity) mapping aliases (`0xp47`), location details, and technical expertise.
  * **Crawler Friendly**: Configured `robots.txt` rules and automated dynamic `sitemap.xml` mapping.
  * **FAQ Structure**: Structured FAQ microdata compliant with schema formats to enhance readability for search engines and AI assistants.
* **Interactive Design**:
  * **Particle Background**: Code symbol background animations that dynamically connect on scroll.
  * **Cursor Aura**: Subtle glowing radial gradient tracking cursor movements.
  * **Smooth Scrolling**: Custom ease-in-out-cubic scroll animations for navigating anchor sections.
  * **Motion Reduction Support**: Respects system preferences (`prefers-reduced-motion`) to disable animations when requested.

---

## рҹӣ пёҸ Technology Stack

* **Core**: Next.js 16 (App Router / Turbopack), React 19, TypeScript
* **Styling**: Tailwind CSS v4, Framer Motion (v12)
* **Icons**: Lucide React, Simple Icons (`react-icons/si`)
* **Fonts**: Geist Sans & Geist Mono (optimized local loading)

---

## рҹ“Ғ Codebase Directory Structure

The components and hooks are organized using Next.js path aliases (`@/*`) to prevent relative directory path nesting:

```text
src/
в”ңв”Җв”Җ app/                                # App Router Configurations
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ favicon.ico
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ globals.css                     # Custom Tailwind Theme Setup
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ layout.tsx                      # Root Layout, Metadata & JSON-LD
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ page.tsx                        # Home Route
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ robots.ts                       # Robots.txt generator
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ sitemap.ts                      # Sitemap.xml generator
в”ңв”Җв”Җ hooks/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ use-safe-reduced-motion.ts      # Media query reduced-motion checker
в”ңв”Җв”Җ components/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ layout/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ site-nav.tsx                # Page header sticky navigation
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ footer.tsx                  # Page copyright footer
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ sections/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ hero-section.tsx            # Landing header & tech marquee loop
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ about-section.tsx           # Bio highlights
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ projects-section.tsx        # Project catalog with category filtering
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ experience-section.tsx      # Chronological history timeline
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ faq-section.tsx             # Factual FAQ schema block
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ contact-section.tsx         # CTAs & mail/linktree redirects
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ shared/
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ section.tsx                 # Shared viewport animate sections wrapper
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ social-link.tsx             # Shared custom social icon button
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ particles-background.tsx    # Shared background code particle canvas
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ portfolio.tsx                   # Main layout compositor
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ providers.tsx                   # Tooltip context provider wrapper
в”ңв”Җв”Җ lib/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ portfolio-data.ts               # Local data model profiles & projects
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ utils.ts                        # Tailwind class mergers (clsx/tailwind-merge)
```

---

## рҹ’» Development Commands

In the project directory, you can run:

### Start Development Server
Starts the Next.js development server with Turbopack enabled on [http://localhost:3000](http://localhost:3000):
```bash
npm run dev
```

### Production Build Check
Compiles the application, runs TypeScript type-checking, and generates optimized static pages:
```bash
npm run build
```

### Run Production Server
Starts the built application in production mode:
```bash
npm run start
```

### Linting Check
Scans the project files for ESLint errors:
```bash
npm run lint
```

name: HireHub-PH
description: A comprehensive online job portal for employers and job seekers, built using PHP, MySQL, and Bootstrap.
readme:
# HireHub PH - Online Job Portal

## Overview
HireHub PH is a comprehensive online job portal designed to cater to both job providers and job seekers. The platform boasts role-based features to ensure a seamless experience for users.

## Technology Stack
- PHP
- MySQL
- HTML5
- CSS3
- JavaScript
- Bootstrap-v4.6

## Steps to Run the Project Locally
1. Install XAMPP.
2. Navigate to `C:/xampp/htdocs/"yourNewFolder"` and paste all the source files.
3. Open the XAMPP control panel, start Apache and MySQL.
4. Visit `localhost` in your browser and access phpMyAdmin.
5. Import the provided SQL database named "onlinejobportal."
6. Open any browser and enter `localhost/"yourNewFolder"` in the URL box.

## Online URL
The website is live at [Hire-Hub.000webhostapp.com](https://Hire-Hub.000webhostapp.com/index.php).

## Key Features

### For Employers:
- Login and Registration functionalities.
- Browse and view all available jobs.
- Apply for jobs by submitting CV files.
- Search for jobs based on category and location.
- View a list of all applied jobs.

### For Employees:
- Login and Registration capabilities.
- Post new job listings.
- Review job applications from seekers.
- Manage and edit posted job listings.
- Access a comprehensive list of existing jobs.
- Search for jobs by category and location.
- Delete or update job posts as needed.
- View individual job posts.
- Download CVs of job seekers.

### Common Features:
- Mobile responsiveness for a seamless user experience.
- Role-based login and registration for tailored access.
- Restricted pages accessible only to authorized users.
- Persistent user login even after page refresh.
- Navbar displays accessible pages based on user roles.
- Role-based access verification for enhanced security.
- Notification system for success and failure through alert boxes.

name: Linkedin-Autopilot
description: Automated LinkedIn Easy Apply tool using Playwright to discover jobs, auto-fill forms, and leverage AI for candidate answers.
readme:
# LinkedIn Autopilot

LinkedIn Easy Apply automation tool built with Playwright that logs in, discovers jobs, auto-fills application forms with profile data and AI-assisted answers, tracks runtime state, and generates session reports.

## Requirements

- Python 3.11+
- Windows PowerShell (recommended for bootstrap scripts)

## Project Layout

```text
src/
  app.py                      # Main orchestration flow
  config.py                   # Loads config/data.json
  logging_setup.py            # Shared Rich logging + console
  models.py                   # SessionStats and JobRecord
  __main__.py                 # python -m src entrypoint
  automation/
    easy_apply_agent.py
    answering_mixin.py
    form_interaction_mixin.py
    modal_submit_mixin.py
  services/
    ai.py
    linkedin.py
    reporting.py
  storage/
    json_store.py
  ui/
    dashboard.py

config/
  data.json                   # Candidate/job preferences (local private data)

assets/
  dummy.pdf                   # Local sample resume (replace with your own)

runtime/
  application_history.json    # Runtime state
  daily_stats.json            # Runtime state
  job_queue.json              # Runtime state
  linkedin_state.json         # Runtime state
  session_report.html         # Generated report output

scripts/
  start.ps1                   # Bootstrap .venv, install deps if needed, run app
  start.cmd                   # cmd wrapper for start.ps1

main.py                       # Root launcher (calls src.app.run)
requirements.txt              # Python dependencies
pyproject.toml                # Packaging metadata
tests/                        # Unit tests
```

## Quick Start (Recommended)

1. Create local config files from the samples:

```powershell
Copy-Item .env.example .env
Copy-Item config/data.sample.json config/data.json
```

Then edit your local `.env` and `config/data.json` with your real values.

2. Run bootstrap script (creates `.venv`, installs requirements if changed, then runs app):

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start.ps1
```

From cmd:

```bat
scripts\start.cmd
```

## Setup Only (No Launch)

Use this when you only want environment + dependency setup:

```powershell
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start.ps1 -NoRun
```

## Manual Run Options

Run with root launcher:

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe main.py
```

Run as module:

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m src
```

Optional console script (after editable install):

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m pip install -e .
linkedin-autopilot
```

## Tests

```powershell
.\.venv\Scripts\python.exe -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test_*.py"
```

## Screenshots

![Session screenshot 1](screenshots/Screenshot%202026-04-18%20144423.png)

![Session screenshot 2](screenshots/Screenshot%202026-04-18%20144545.png)

## Configuration

- Edit your local `.env` and `config/data.json` with your own values.
- Set `application_settings.resume_path` to your resume file path (for example `assets/your_resume.pdf`).
- Runtime/session files are stored in `runtime/`.

## Run

- Windows quick start: `powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts/start.ps1`
- Manual run: `python main.py`
- Module run: `python -m src`

name: moe-llm-from-scratch
description: A PyTorch implementation of a Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) Language Model built from scratch, featuring SwiGLU activation, RMSNorm, custom byte-level BPE tokenizer, and a ReAct reasoning agent.
readme:
# Sparse MoE LLM From Scratch рҹҢҢ

**A Custom PyTorch Implementation of a Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Large Language Model**
_Developed by Jay Patrick Cano (0x3ef8)_

![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Proprietary-red)
![Architecture](https://img.shields.io/badge/Architecture-Sparse_MoE-blue)
![Activation](https://img.shields.io/badge/Activation-SwiGLU-green)
![Norm](https://img.shields.io/badge/Norm-RMSNorm-purple)

**Aether-1.0-MoE** is a cutting-edge, "from-scratch" implementation of a Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Large Language Model. Unlike standard educational implementations, Aether utilizes **2025/2026 State-of-the-Art (SOTA)** architectural patterns found in models like **Gemini 1.5** and **Llama 3**.

---

## вҡЎ Technical Highlights

AetherAI is engineered for **Reasoning** and **Efficiency**:

- **рҹ§  Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE)**: Implements routed "Experts" (Top-2 Gating) to decouple model size from inference cost. High intelligence, low latency.
- **рҹ“җ SwiGLU Activation**: Replaces standard GELU with Swish-Gated Linear Units, increasing reasoning capability per parameter (matches PaLM/Llama 3).
- **вҡ–пёҸ RMSNorm**: Replaces LayerNorm with Root Mean Square Normalization for superior training stability at depth.
- **рҹ—ЈпёҸ Custom Tokenizer**: Includes a dedicated Byte-Level BPE tokenizer trained heavily on domain data (Vocab: 441), offering 100x efficiency over generic tokenizers.
- **рҹӨ– ReAct Agent**: Features a "System 2" thinking agent that reasons `(Thought -> Action -> Observation)` before answering.

---

## рҹҸ—пёҸ Architecture

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Input Query] --> B(Custom BPE Tokenizer)
    B --> C{Agent Core}
    C -->|Thinking| D[Chain-of-Thought Loop]
    D --> E[Transformer Backbone]
    E --> F[RMSNorm]
    F --> G[MoE Block]
    G -->|Router| H[Expert 1: SwiGLU]
    G -->|Router| I[Expert 2: SwiGLU]
    H & I --> J[Output Logits]
    J --> K[Response]
```

---

## рҹҡҖ Quick Start

### 1. Prerequisites

- Python 3.10+
- PyTorch 2.0+ (CUDA Recommended)

### 2. Installation

```bash
git clone git@github.com:0x3EF8/AetherAI.git
cd AetherAI
pip install -r requirements.txt  # or pip install -e .
```

### 3. Training Pipeline

To achieve the "Aether-1.0" class performance, you must complete the training cycle:

**Step A: Train the Vocabulary**

```bash
python scripts/train_tokenizer.py
```

_Generates optimized `data/tokenizer.json`._

**Step B: Train the Brain**

```bash
python scripts/train.py train.epochs=20
```

_Uses Hydra configuration `configs/model/aether_moe.yaml`. Saves to `checkpoints/best_model.pt`._

### 4. Deployment

**Option A: The Workbench (Web UI)**
Run the two distributed components:

1.  **Neural Server (API)**: `python scripts/server.py`
2.  **Client Interface (Streamlit)**: `python -m streamlit run scripts/app.py`

**Option B: The Terminal (Hacker Mode)**

```bash
python scripts/chat.py
```

---

## рҹ“Ӯ Project Structure

- `src/aether/models/`: **The Physics**. Contains `transformer.py` (RMSNorm/SwiGLU) and `moe_layer.py`.
- `src/aether/core/`: **The Mind**. Contains `agent.py` (ReAct Logic).
- `configs/`: **The DNA**. Hydra definitions for hyper-parameters.
- `scripts/`: **The Tools**. Application logic for Training, Serving, and Chat.

---

## рҹӣЎпёҸ License

Proprietary software developed by **0x3ef8**.
All rights reserved 2026.

name: Unified-API-Server
description: An auto-loading FastAPI service hub providing unified endpoints for Edge TTS, yt-dlp video downloads, and QR generation.
readme:
# Unified API Server

FastAPI server with auto-loading service architecture. Drop a folder into `services/` вҖ” zero configuration required.

## Services

| Service | Endpoint | Description |
|---------|----------|-------------|
| **TTS** | `POST /tts` | Text-to-speech via Microsoft Edge Neural TTS |
| **Download** | `POST /unidl` | Video/audio download via yt-dlp |
| **QR Code** | `POST /qr` | QR code generation (PNG/SVG/PDF/EPS/TXT) via segno |

## Quick Start

```bash
pip install -r requirements.txt
python main.py
```

Server starts at `http://localhost:8000`. Override with `API_PORT=3000 python main.py`.

## Usage

### TTS вҖ” `POST /tts`

Generate audio:
```json
{"text": "Hello world", "voice": "en-US-JennyNeural", "rate": "+0%", "pitch": "+0Hz"}
```

List all available voices:
```json
{"list_voices": true}
```

### Download вҖ” `POST /unidl`

```json
{"url": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dQw4w9WgXcQ", "quality": "720p"}
```

Returns the file directly with auto-cleanup.

### QR Code вҖ” `POST /qr`

Standard QR:
```json
{"data": "https://example.com", "format": "png"}
```

WiFi QR:
```json
{"ssid": "MyNetwork", "password": "secret123", "security": "WPA"}
```

## Project Structure

```
в”ңв”Җв”Җ main.py              # Entry point
в”ңв”Җв”Җ config.py            # Server settings (host, port, debug)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ utils.py             # Shared utilities (internet check)
в”ңв”Җв”Җ cache/               # All service runtime data
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ tts/             # TTS audio cache
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ yt_dlp/          # Download temp files
в”ңв”Җв”Җ services/
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ __init__.py      # Auto-loader (ServiceLoader)
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ edge_tts/        # TTS service
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ config.py    # TTS settings
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ models.py    # Request models
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ engine.py    # Generation engine
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ cache.py     # Audio cache
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ endpoints.py # API route
в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ yt_dlp/          # Download service
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ config.py    # Download settings
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ models.py    # Request models
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ utils.py     # FFmpeg check, URL validation
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ dependencies.py # FFmpeg auto-download
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ downloader.py   # Download engine with retry
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ config_builder.py # yt-dlp options builder
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в”ңв”Җв”Җ formats.py   # Quality presets
в”Ӯ   в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ endpoints.py # API route
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ qr/              # QR service
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ models.py    # Request models
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ generator.py # QR engine
в”Ӯ       в””в”Җв”Җ endpoints.py # API route
в””в”Җв”Җ tests/               # pytest suite
```

## Adding a Service

Create `services/myservice/endpoints.py`:

```python
from fastapi import APIRouter

router = APIRouter(prefix="/myservice", tags=["MyService"])

@router.get("/hello")
async def hello():
    return {"message": "Hello!"}
```

Restart the server. Done.

## Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `API_HOST` | `0.0.0.0` | Server host |
| `API_PORT` | `8000` | Server port |
| `API_DEBUG` | `false` | Debug mode (auto-reload) |
| `TTS_CACHE_ENABLED` | `true` | Enable TTS audio caching |
| `TTS_DEFAULT_VOICE` | `en-US-AnaNeural` | Default TTS voice |
| `TTS_MAX_TEXT_LENGTH` | `5000` | Max text length for TTS |
| `YTDLP_RETRY_ATTEMPTS` | `3` | Download retry attempts |
| `YTDLP_SOCKET_TIMEOUT` | `30` | Download socket timeout (s) |

## Testing

```bash
pytest
```

name: windows-user-profile-renamer
description: A safe, automated PowerShell toolkit with an interactive batch control panel interface to rename a Windows user profile folder, update registry SID mappings, and create compatibility junctions.
readme:
# Windows User Profile Renamer рҹӣ пёҸ

This repository provides a safe, step-by-step PowerShell-automated solution to rename a Windows user profile directory and its associated user account.

---

## The Problem
When you rename a Windows user account via the Control Panel or Settings, Windows renames the *display name* and the *login name*, but **it does not rename the user's profile folder** (e.g., `C:\Users\oldname` remains unchanged). 

Manually renaming this directory is highly risky because:
1. The folder is locked by active system processes while you are logged in.
2. Direct renaming breaks registry mappings, causing Windows to load a temporary profile instead.
3. Hardcoded paths inside applications (app data, settings) will break.

## The Solution
This toolkit automates the renaming process cleanly. It is composed of three scripts and a main control panel:
* **[control_panel.bat](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/control_panel.bat)**: The unified command-line control panel to manage the entire workflow.
* **[create_temp_admin.ps1](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/scripts/create_temp_admin.ps1)**: Provisions a temporary administrator account.
* **[rename_profile.ps1](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/scripts/rename_profile.ps1)**: Performs the profile directory rename, registry path update, and creates a directory junction for backward compatibility.
* **[remove_temp_admin.ps1](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/scripts/remove_temp_admin.ps1)**: Cleans up the temporary administrator account when finished.


---

## рҹ“Ӣ Prerequisites & Warnings

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Files Shared Location**: Because you must log out of your current account and log into the `temp` account, the scripts **must** be stored in a shared directory that both accounts can access. 
> 
> Please copy/move the entire toolkit folder to:
> `C:\Users\Public\windows-user-profile-renamer`
> *(The toolkit has already been automatically prepared in this directory for you!)*

> [!WARNING]
> Renaming user profile directories modifies registry keys and system paths. **Ensure you back up any critical files before running the script.**

* **PowerShell Execution Policy**: You must allow running scripts. You can enable this temporarily by running:
  ```powershell
  Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope Process -ExecutionPolicy Bypass
  ```
* **Administrator Privileges**: All scripts must be run as Administrator (the `control_panel.bat` script automatically requests admin privilege elevation).

---

## рҹҡҖ Step-by-Step Guide

### Step 0: Clone the Repository and Copy to Public Directory (Crucial Setup)
Because Windows will sign you out of your current account to rename it, the scripts **must** be placed in a public directory that both your current account and the new temporary account can access.

1. Open PowerShell and clone the repository:
   ```powershell
   git clone https://github.com/0xp47/windows-user-profile-renamer.git
   ```
2. Copy the cloned folder into the Public folder:
   ```powershell
   Copy-Item -Path .\windows-user-profile-renamer -Destination C:\Users\Public\windows-user-profile-renamer -Recurse -Force
   ```
   *(Or manually copy the folder and paste it into `C:\Users\Public\` so that the path becomes `C:\Users\Public\windows-user-profile-renamer`)*


---

### Step 1: Create the Temporary Administrator Account
1. Open the shared folder: `C:\Users\Public\windows-user-profile-renamer`
2. Double-click **[control_panel.bat](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/control_panel.bat)**.
3. Select Option `1` (Create Temporary Admin).
4. This creates a temporary user named `temp` with the password `123456` and grants it administrative privileges.


---

### Step 2: Switch to the Temporary Account
1. Save your work and **Sign Out** of your current account (`0x3EF8`).
2. On the Windows sign-in screen, select the **temp** account.
3. Log in using the password: `123456`.
4. Wait for Windows to finish setting up the temporary desktop (this takes about a minute).

---

### Step 3: Run the Renamer Script
1. Once logged into the `temp` account, open File Explorer and go to:
   `C:\Users\Public\windows-user-profile-renamer`
2. Run **[control_panel.bat](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/control_panel.bat)**.
3. Select Option `2` (Run Profile Renamer).

#### What the script does:
* **Account Rename**: Renames the local user account from `0x3EF8` to `0xp47`.
* **Profile Folder Rename**: Renames `C:\Users\0x3EF8` to `C:\Users\0xp47`.
* **Registry Update**: Locates the Security Identifier (SID) of the account and updates the profile registry key path (`ProfileImagePath`) in `HKLM:\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\ProfileList`.
* **Junction Creation**: Creates a directory junction pointing `C:\Users\0x3EF8` to `C:\Users\0xp47`. This is critical: if any legacy applications have hardcoded paths to your old profile name, they will resolve seamlessly to the new location.

---

### Step 4: Log back in and Clean up
1. **Sign Out** of the `temp` account.
2. Sign in to your newly renamed account: **0xp47**.
3. Open the shared folder: `C:\Users\Public\windows-user-profile-renamer`
4. Run **[control_panel.bat](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/control_panel.bat)**.
5. Select Option `3` (Remove Temporary Admin).
   * This safely deletes the `temp` user account and completely removes its temporary profile directory and associated registry values.

## рҹӣ пёҸ Script Customization
If you need to rename a different user account, open **[rename_profile.ps1](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/scripts/rename_profile.ps1)** and edit the following values at the top of the file:

```powershell
$oldName = "0x3EF8"  # The current username
$newName = "0xp47"   # The desired username
```

---

## рҹ“Ғ Repository Structure
* **[control_panel.bat](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/control_panel.bat)**: Main interactive Control Panel CLI launcher.
* **scripts/**: Subfolder containing core PowerShell scripts.
  * **[rename_profile.ps1](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/scripts/rename_profile.ps1)**: Main renamer utility.
  * **[create_temp_admin.ps1](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/scripts/create_temp_admin.ps1)**: Tool to set up the temporary profile `temp` with password `123456`.
  * **[remove_temp_admin.ps1](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/scripts/remove_temp_admin.ps1)**: Script to remove the temporary admin and clean up folders.
* **[README.md](file:///C:/Users/Public/windows-user-profile-renamer/README.md)**: Documentation and step-by-step instructions.

name: XAMPP-Repair-Tool
description: An automated PowerShell-backed CLI recovery tool to diagnose, resolve port conflicts, and repair corrupted MySQL/Apache in XAMPP.
readme:
# XAMPP Repair Engine

An automated PowerShell-backed CLI utility designed to diagnose, isolate, and repair corrupted XAMPP MySQL/Apache installations on Windows. It fixes common startup crashes, resolves port conflicts, and optimizes database performance safely.

---

## рҹ“– Complete Documentation

A comprehensive guide explaining the internal mechanics, port resolution, system restoration, and recovery modes can be found in the [XAMPP Repair Guide](XAMPP_Repair_Guide.md).

---

## рҹҡҖ Features

*   **Quick Repair Protocol:** Instantly purges locked `.pid` files, corrupt `aria_log_control` files, and mismatched `ib_logfile*` logs to clear process hangs.
*   **Advanced System Recovery (Smart Rebuild):** 
    *   **Port Conflict Scan & Kill:** Scans ports 80, 443, and 3306, automatically killing conflicting process threads (Skype, VMware, orphaned processes).
    *   **Tablespace Transplantation:** Replaces corrupted system databases (`mysql`, `performance_schema`, `phpmyadmin`) from XAMPP templates while preserving your main `ibdata1` shared tablespace.
    *   **Progressive Safe Boot:** If rebuild fails, injects `innodb_force_recovery` modes (1 to 3) to launch MySQL in Read-Only mode so you can export databases via phpMyAdmin.
*   **Table Optimization:** Runs `mysqlcheck` automatically to repair, defragment, and optimize all active schemas.
*   **Data Preservation & Factory Reset:** Create manual snapshots of the data folder at any time or execute a fresh factory reset state when files are unsalvageable.

## рҹӣ пёҸ Menu Selection Overview

1.  **Quick Repair Protocol (Cache/Lock Purge):** Wipes locks and temporary logs. Databases are left untouched.
2.  **Advanced System Recovery (Deep Clean + Rebuild):** Rebuilds system databases and imports user schemas. *Note: Resets root password to blank (no password).*
3.  **Optimize Database Tables (Defrag/Repair):** Runs database checks and table optimizations.
4.  **Data Preservation (Manual Snapshot):** Safely backs up the `mysql/data` folder to a timestamped folder.
5.  **Heuristic Log Analysis (Error Stream):** Displays colored log outputs from `mysql_error.log`.
6.  **EMERGENCY FACTORY RESET:** Performs a clean restore from default XAMPP backups. Wipes current databases (creates quarantine first).
7.  **Terminate Session:** Safely exits the session.

## вҡ пёҸ Requirements & Execution

1.  **Place the Script:** Place `Repair-Tool BETA.bat` in your project folder (expects default XAMPP at `C:\xampp`).
2.  **Run as Administrator:** Right-click `Repair-Tool BETA.bat` and select **Run as Administrator** (required for process isolation and stopping service threads).
